summary: iter266.12 — Chef Outlet Dashboard + MaestroBQT BEO Month-Timeline. 20/20 pytest cases PASS against deployed preview URL. All 6 new chef_outlet endpoints validated (dashboard envelope, MC P10<=P50<=P90 invariant across all 4 iteration counts {1000,2000,5000,7500} and all 4 horizons {d1,d3,d5,d7}, invalid iter falls back to 2000, found:false for missing outlet, outlets-for-chef with/without email filter, forecast/recalibrate + persistence + accuracy read-back, beo-timeline color_tag set + revenue/cost formulas $85*covers and 32%*revenue, cumulative multi-select with and without events). iter266.11 Labor Brain Rail regression also clean.
critical_code_review_comments: chef_outlet.py is ~898 LOC — borderline of the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into chef_outlet/dashboard.py, forecast.py, beo_timeline.py modules in a future refactor for maintainability.

POST /beo-timeline/cumulative accepts a raw List[str] body — works but unconventional. Wrapping in Pydantic model (e.g. {event_ids: [...]}) would give better OpenAPI docs and request validation errors.

_monte_carlo_forecast invalid-iterations fallback to 2000 is implicit (no warning surfaced in response). Consider echoing requested_iterations vs applied_iterations so the chef knows their input was coerced.

is_recent_change relies on string-compare of ISO datetimes (updated_at >= recent_threshold). Works because both are UTC ISO strings, but a parsed datetime compare would be more robust if timezone formats ever drift.
Estimated revenue is hard-coded $85/cover and 32% food-cost target. Acceptable for v1 but should become outlet-tunable config (mentioned in code comment as 'chef-tunable').
seed_data_creation: Test created 1 chef_forecast_feedback row tagged note='TEST_iter266_12' (outlet=p66demo-galley, predicted=10000, actual=11000). Safe to leave or clean via prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 chef_outlet endpoints + 2 echo-schedule regression endpoints are green. The dashboard endpoint takes ~1-2s per call (Monte Carlo at 7500 iters x4 horizons). beo_functions collection has events for current month, so cumulative test exercised real IDs. Next iterations should reuse this test file as a regression suite.
